question.label: Voice leverage
question.name: When you say 'where my voice has most leverage,' what feels unclear there?
question.description: Drills into the leverage uncertainty she named to surface the specific clarity gap behind her quieter presence.

answer: Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.

assessment.note: She disclosed tiredness from constant context switching, which signals strong drag on engagement; also surfaced a wellbeing strain worth probing next. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'constant context switching' could be picked up next session if needed.

new_queue.label: Landing context
new_queue.name: Before we get specific, what feels most useful to talk through today?

new_queue.label: Scope strain
new_queue.name: What part of your current scope feels heaviest right now?

new_queue.label: Tradeoffs now
new_queue.name: What are you choosing to say yes to most deliberately right now?

new_queue.label: Manager support
new_queue.name: What would you want more of from me right now?

new_queue.label: Next-step focus
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to focus first?

history.answer_excerpt: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.
